title: System and method for handling image data
abstract: A data processing unit receives a reference image (IMG1 3D ) of a deformable physical entity, a target image (IMG2 3D ) of said physical entity, and a first region of interest (ROI1 3D ) defining a first volume in the reference image (IMG1 3D ) representing a reference image element. The reference image (IMG1 3D ), the target image (IMG2 3D ) and the first region of interest (ROI1 3D ) all contain 3D datasets. In response to user commands (c1; c2), the data processing unit defines a first contour (C1 2D ) in a first plane through the target image (IMG2 3D ), which is presented to a user via a display unit together with graphic data reflecting the reference image (IMG1 3D ), the target image (IMG2 3D ) and the first region of interest (ROI1 3D ). The first contour (C1 2D ) is aligned with at least a portion of a first border (IEB1) of a target image element (IE 3D ) in the target image (IMG2 3D ). The target image element (IE 3D ) corresponds to the reference image element in the reference image (IMG1 3D ). Based on the first contour (C1 2D ), the target image (IMG2 3D ) and the first region of interest (ROI1 3D ); the data processing unit determines a second region of interest (ROI2 3D ) defining a second volume in the target image (IMG2 3D ).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. An image handling system (100), comprising: a data processing unit (110) configured to receive: a reference image (IMG13D) of a deformable physical entity, a target image (IMG23D) of said physical entity, and a first region of interest (ROH3D) defining a first volume in the reference image (IMGI3D), which first volume represents a reference image element, the reference image (IMGI3D), the target image (IMG23D) and the first region of interest (ROH3D) containing a respective three-dimensional dataset; at least one data input unit (131, 132) configured to receive user commands (c1; c2); and a display unit (140) configured to present graphic data (GD) reflecting the reference image (IMGI3D), the target image (IMG2 3 D) and the first region of interest (ROH3D), characterized in that the data processing unit (110) is further configured to: define, in response to the user commands (c1; c2), a first contour (C12D) in a first plane through the target image (IMG23D), the first contour (C12D) being aligned with at least a portion of a first border (IEB1) of a target image element (IE3D) in the target image (IMG23D), the target image element (IE3D) corresponding to the reference image element in the reference image (IMGI3D); and determine a second region of interest (ROI23D) defining a second volume in the target image (IMG23D), the second region of interest (ROI23D) being determined based on the first contour (C12D), the target image (IMG23D) and the first region of interest (ROMSD). 2. The image handling system (100) according to claim 1, wherein the data processing unit (110) is further configured to compute a vector field (VFiâ2) describing a relationship between the first region of interest (ROH3D) and the second region of interest (ROI23D), the second region of interest (ROI23D) being obtainable by transforming the first region of interest (ROH3D) via the vector 

 field (VFi â2 ). 3. The image handling system (100) according to claim 1 , wherein data processing unit (1 10) is further configured to: generate the second region of interest (RO I23D) based on the first region of interest (RO I 13D) and the vector field (VFi â 2); and produce graphic data (GP) for presentation on the display unit (140), which graphic data (GP) reflect the second region of interest (ROI23D) overlaid on the target image (I MG23D) . 4. The image handling system (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein data processing unit (1 10) is further configured to: receive additional user commands (c1 ; c2), and in response thereto, define a second contour (C22D) in a second plane through the target image (I MG23D), the second contour (C22D) being aligned with at least a portion of a second border (I EB2) of the target image element (I E3D) in the target image (I MG23D); and determine the second region of interest (RO I23D) on the further basis of the second contour (C2 2 D) . 5. The image handling system (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein data processing unit (1 10) is configured to determine the second region of interest (RO I23D) based on a non-linear optimization algorithm applied to the first contour (C12D) and an intersection between the second region of interest (RO I23D) and the first plane, the non-linear optimization algorithm being configured to penalize deviation of the second region of interest (ROI23D) from the first contour (C12D) . 6. The image handling system (100) according to claim 5, wherein the second region of interest (RO I23D) is represented by a tri- angular mesh, and the non-linear optimization algorithm involves: computing a set of intersection points between the second region of interest (RO I23D) and the first plane, each intersection 

 point (VO) in the set of intersection points being computed by means of a convex combination of eight voxel centers (V1 , V2, V3, V4, V5, V6, V7, V8) being adjacent to the intersection point (VO) using mean value coordinates; and applying a two-dimensional distance transform on a Euclidean distance between each computed intersection point and the first contour (C12D). 7. The image handling system (100) according to claim 5, wherein the second region of interest (RO I23D) is represented by a tri- angular mesh, and the non-linear optimization algorithm involves: computing a set of intersection points between the second region of interest (RO I23D) and the first plane, and for each intersection point in the set of intersection points; determining a normal projection from the second region of interest (RO I23D) towards the first plane, the normal projection extending in an interval of predetermined length, and if within the predetermined length the normal intersects with the first contour (C12D) at a juncture, the juncture is included as a tentative delimitation point of an updated second region of interest; and repeating the determining step based on the updated second region of interest until a stop criterion is fulfilled. 8. A method of handling images, the method comprising: receiving a reference image (I MG13D) of a deformable physical entity; receiving a target image (I MG23D) of said physical entity; receiving a first region of interest (ROH 3D) defining a first volume in the reference image (IMG I 3D), which first volume represents a reference image element, the reference image (I MG13D), the target image (I MG23D) and the first region of interest (ROH 3D) containing a respective three-dimensional dataset; receiving user commands (c1 ; c2) via at least one data input unit (131 , 132); and presenting graphic data (G D) on a display unit (140), the graphic data (GD) reflecting the reference image (I MGI 3D), the 

 target image (IMG23D) and the first region of interest (ROI13D), characterized by the method comprising: defining, in response to the user commands (c1 ; c2), a first contour (C12D) in a first plane through the target image (IMG23D), the first contour (C12D) being aligned with at least a portion of a first border (IEB1) of a target image element (IE3D) in the target image (IMG23D), the target image element (IE3D) corresponding to the reference image element in the reference image (IMGI3D); and determining a second region of interest (ROI23D) defining a second volume in the target image (IMG23D), the second region of interest (ROI23D) being determined based on the first contour (C12D), the target image (IMG23D) and the first region of interest (ROMSD). 9. The method according to claim 8, further comprising computing a vector field (VFiâ2) describing a relationship between the first region of interest (ROH3D) and the second region of interest (ROI23D), the second region of interest (ROI23D) being obtainable by transforming the first region of interest (ROH3D) via the vector field (VFi â2 ). 10. The method according to claim 8, further comprising: generating the second region of interest (ROI23D) based on the first region of interest (ROH3D) and the vector field (VFiâ2); and producing graphic data (GP) for presentation on the display unit (140), which graphic data (GP) reflect the second region of interest (ROI23D) overlaid on the target image (IMG23D). 11. The method according to any one of the claims 8 to 10, further comprising: receiving additional user commands (c1; c2), and in response thereto, defining a second contour (C22D) in a second plane through the target image (IMG23D), the second contour (C22D) being aligned with at least a portion of a second border (IEB2) of the 

 target image element (IE3D) in the target image (IMG23D); and determining the second region of interest (ROI23D) on the further basis of the second contour (C22D). 12. The method according to any one of claims 8 to 11, com- prising determining the second region of interest (ROI23D) based on a non-linear optimization algorithm applied to the first contour (C12D) and an intersection between the second region of interest (ROI23D) and the first plane, the non-linear optimization algorithm being configured to penalize deviation of the second region of interest (ROI23D) from the first contour (C12D). 13. The method according to claim 12, wherein the second region of interest (ROI23D) is represented by a triangular mesh, and the method involves: computing a set of intersection points between the second region of interest (ROI23D) and the first plane, each intersection point (V0) in the set of intersection points being computed by means of a convex combination of eight voxel centers (V1, V2, V3, V4, V5, V6, V7, V8) being adjacent to the intersection point (V0) using mean value coordinates; and applying a two-dimensional distance transform on a Euclidean distance between each computed intersection point and the first contour (C12D). 14. The method according to claim 12, wherein the second region of interest (ROI23D) is represented by a triangular mesh, and the method involves: computing a set of intersection points between the second region of interest (ROI23D) and the first plane, and for each intersection point in the set of intersection points; determining a normal projection from the second region of interest (ROI23D) towards the first plane, the normal projection extending in an interval of predetermined length, and if within the predetermined length the normal intersects with the first contour (C12D) at a juncture, the juncture is included as a tentative delimi- 

 tation point of an updated second region of interest; and repeating the determining step based on the updated second region of interest until a stop criterion is fulfilled. 15. A computer program product (SW) loadable into a memory (1 15) of at least one processor, comprising software for performing the method according to any of claims 8 to 14 when executed on the at least one processor. 16. A processor-readable medium (1 15) comprising instructions which, when executed by at least one processor, cause the at least one processor to perform the method according to any one of claims 8 to 14.
